NCT02418585.name: TRANSFORM-2
NCT02418585.group: TRD adults 18-64, intranasal esketamine 56-84 mg + oral AD vs intranasal placebo + oral AD (4 wk, MADRS MD)

allOutcomes.title: MADRS total score change from baseline at day 28 (primary, esketamine minus placebo, MD)

NCT02418585.snippet: Source: Popova V et al. Am J Psychiatry 2019;176:428-438 (TRANSFORM-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 223 adults aged 18-64 with treatment-resistant depression (DSM-5 MDD, non-response to >=2 adequate antidepressant trials in current episode) randomized 1:1 double-blind to intranasal esketamine 56 mg on day 1 then 56 or 84 mg twice weekly PLUS a newly initiated oral antidepressant (n=114), vs matched intranasal placebo PLUS oral antidepressant (n=109), for 4 weeks (induction phase).

evidence.label: Primary - MADRS at Day 28
evidence.text: MADRS total score change from baseline at day 28: esketamine -19.8 vs placebo -15.8; least-squares mean treatment difference -4.0 (95% CI -7.3 to -0.6; P=0.020). Primary endpoint met under mixed-effects model for repeated measures.

evidence.label: Secondary - Response, Remission, Safety
evidence.text: MADRS >=50% response at day 28: esketamine 69.3% vs placebo 52.0% (NNT ~6). MADRS <=12 remission: 52.5% vs 31.0%. Dissociation 26% (transient, peri-dosing), nausea, dizziness, vertigo more common with esketamine; no clinically meaningful cognitive impairment at long-term. Two deaths (neither drug-related).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.source: Popova 2019 Am J Psychiatry design + RoB 2.0
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IWRS randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind with matched intranasal placebo. D3 LOW - 90% completion. D4 LOW - blinded MADRS raters, central rater training. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 64 Years; healthy volunteers: f. NCT02422186.name: TRANSFORM-3
NCT02422186.group: TRD adults >=65, intranasal esketamine + oral AD vs placebo + oral AD (4 wk, MADRS MD)

allOutcomes.title: MADRS total score change from baseline at day 28 (primary, esketamine minus placebo, MD)

NCT02422186.snippet: Source: Ochs-Ross R et al. Am J Geriatr Psychiatry 2020;28:121-141 (TRANSFORM-3).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.source: Ochs-Ross 2020 Am J Geriatr Psychiatry, CONSORT
evidence.text: 137 adults aged >=65 with treatment-resistant depression randomized 1:1 double-blind to flexibly-dosed intranasal esketamine 28-84 mg twice weekly PLUS newly initiated oral antidepressant (n=72), vs intranasal placebo PLUS oral antidepressant (n=65), for 4 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - MADRS at Day 28
evidence.source: Ochs-Ross 2020 Am J Geriatr Psychiatry, Figure 2
evidence.text: MADRS change at day 28: esketamine -10.0 vs placebo -6.3; least-squares mean treatment difference -3.6 (95% CI -7.2 to -0.07; P=0.029 one-sided pre-specified, though CI includes zero on two-sided test). Modest but statistically supportive primary result in older adults.

evidence.label: Secondary - Response, Subgroup by Age
evidence.source: Ochs-Ross 2020 Am J Geriatr Psychiatry, Table 2
evidence.text: MADRS response at day 28: esketamine 27% vs placebo 13%. Effect larger in 65-74 age-subgroup than >=75. Safety: dissociation 21% (less than younger adults), nausea, dizziness, systolic BP increases peri-dosing. No falls or cognitive worsening signal.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.source: Ochs-Ross 2020 Am J Geriatr Psychiatry design + RoB 2.0
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IWRS. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 88% completion. D4 LOW - blinded MADRS raters. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 65 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* At the time of signing the informed consent form (ICF), participant must be a man or woman 65 years of age or older~* At the start of the Screening/prospective observational Phase, participant must meet the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-5) diagnostic criteria for single-episode major depressive disorder (MDD) [if single-episode MDD, the duration must be greater than or equal to (>=) 2 years] or recurrent MDD, without psychotic features, base...

title: RapidMeta Psychiatry | Intranasal Esketamine for Treatment-Resistant Depression v1.1
